questions.prompt: For $0 \leq t \leq 120$, the temperature of a bowl of soup at time $t$ is modeled by the function $S$ that satisfies the differential equation $\frac{d S}{d t}=-\frac{1}{20}(S-68)$, where $S(t)$ is measured in degrees Fahrenheit ( ${ }^{\circ} \mathrm{F}$ ) and $t$ is measured in minutes. The temperature of the bowl of soup is always greater than $68^{\circ} \mathrm{F}$ and is $148^{\circ} \mathrm{F}$ at time $t=0$.

options.text: Explain why the following could not be a slope field for the differential equation $\frac{d S}{d t}=-\frac{1}{20}(S-68)$. - The slope field consists of short line segments representing the slope at various points on the plane. - The line segments are horizontal at $S$ equals 68. - As $S$ increases, the line segments become increasingly steep upward and to the right. - As t increases, the behavior of the line segments is unchanged.
options.html: Explain why the following could not be a slope field for the differential equation $\frac{d S}{d t}=-\frac{1}{20}(S-68)$. - The slope field consists of short line segments representing the slope at various points on the plane. - The line segments are horizontal at $S$ equals 68. - As $S$ increases, the line segments become increasingly steep upward and to the right. - As t increases, the behavior of the line segments is unchanged.

options.text: Use the line tangent to the graph of $S$ at $t=0$ to approximate the temperature of the soup at time $t=5$ minutes.
options.html: Use the line tangent to the graph of $S$ at $t=0$ to approximate the temperature of the soup at time $t=5$ minutes.

options.text: According to the model, is the approximation found in part B an overestimate or an underestimate of the temperature of the soup at time $t=5$ minutes? Give a reason for your answer.
options.html: According to the model, is the approximation found in part B an overestimate or an underestimate of the temperature of the soup at time $t=5$ minutes? Give a reason for your answer.

options.text: Using separation of variables, find the particular solution $y=S(t)$ to the differential equation $\frac{d S}{d t}=-\frac{1}{20}(S-68)$ with initial condition $S(0)=148$.
options.html: Using separation of variables, find the particular solution $y=S(t)$ to the differential equation $\frac{d S}{d t}=-\frac{1}{20}(S-68)$ with initial condition $S(0)=148$.

options.text: Determine whether $f$ has a relative minimum, a relative maximum, or neither at $x=0$. Give a reason for your answer.

options.text: The first two nonzero terms of the power series for $f$ are used to approximate $f\left(\frac{1}{2}\right)$. Use the alternating series error bound to determine an upper bound on the error of the approximation.
